command: Use @skill:chained-vulnerability-static-audit to perform a static-only chained vulnerability review of this codebase. Inspect only the current working directory. Do not use live probes, dynamic scanners, shell commands, or files outside this workspace. Write the final report with write_chained_vulnerability_report to docs/security/CHAINED_VULNERABILITIES_REVIEW.md.

tool_calls.arguments_summary: {"action": "add", "text": "Phase 1: Attack Surface Mapping - identify all public routes, API endpoints, user-controlled inputs"}
{"action": "add", "text": "Phase 2: Weakness Inventory - identify all standalone and chained weaknesses"}
{"action": "add", "text": "Phase 3: Attack Graph Synthesis - connect sources to weaknesses to sinks"}
{"action": "add", "text": "Phase 4: Write final report with all chains identified"}

## Dockerfile
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn package -DskipTests

FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8082
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

chains.chain_name: LDAP Injection → Directory Structure Disclosure → Inventory Tampering

unmatched_candidate_chain_titles: Chain 2: SSRF via Shipping Label URL → Internal Service Access → Data Exfiltration
Chain 3: Hardcoded Frontend Credentials → Default Credential Compromise
Chain 4: IDOR on Inventory Adjust + Missing Authorization → Inventory Manipulation
Chain 5: Verbose Error Handling → Application Fingerprinting → Attack Facilitation
Chain 6: Exposed Actuator + Config Display → Full Application Recon → Attack Amplification
